denial_pattern_sources: peer_to_peer_window_verbal_only: AMA 2023 Prior Authorization Physician Survey; documented in multiple state PA reform laws (TX HB 2002, VA SB 1272)
denial_letter_text:
Dear Member,

We received your provider's request on 02/06/2026 for Venetoclax plus obinutuzumab related to Chronic lymphocytic leukemia, symptomatic (C91.10). After review, we cannot approve this request.

Our review applied UnitedHealthcare Medical Management Guidelines and Optum clinical criteria. The documentation submitted does not meet medical necessity criteria because the service is not medically necessary for this member at this time. The requested frequency exceeds plan guidelines for Chronic lymphocytic leukemia, symptomatic (C91.10). Additional visits are not medically necessary at this cadence.

Specifically, the information provided does not satisfy the utilization-management requirements associated with frequency excessive. The clinical notes reviewed did not include sufficient detail to confirm that plan criteria were met. The requesting provider may submit additional records, but the current file does not support approval.

If your provider wishes to discuss this determination, they may contact our physician review line. Any discussion must occur within the verbal timeframe communicated to office staff (not confirmed in this letter).

You have the right to appeal this determination. To file a standard appeal, send a written request and supporting medical records within 180 days of the date of this letter. Include your member identification number and the service reference shown on your explanation of benefits.

Sincerely,

UnitedHealthcare Utilization Management

Please note that benefits are subject to all terms, exclusions, and limitations of your group health plan document. This letter constitutes the formal adverse determination for the service identified above.
clinical_context: The 49-year-old member has Chronic lymphocytic leukemia, symptomatic (C91.10). She has been treated under commercial coverage with Venetoclax plus obinutuzumab recommended as the next appropriate step. WBC 85k, hemoglobin 10.2 g/dL, bulky nodes; Binet stage C symptoms. This directly contradicts the insurer's position that frequency excessive bars coverage: the chart documents dates, doses, and objective findings that were submitted with the original request submitted in Q1 2026 but are not reflected in the denial letter's rationale. Treating clinicians note ongoing functional impairment and avoidable harm if her care is delayed further.

insurer_voice.reasoning: Letter reads as authentic UHC correspondence with UnitedHealthcare medical policy and procedural tone.
